title: Tablet Offers High Risk, High Reward (InformationWeek on Microsoft Tablet PC launch)
author: InformationWeek (cover story; Dec 17, 2001 issue, page 49)
abstract: InformationWeek's December 17 2001 cover story on Microsoft's Tablet PC launch led by distinguished engineer Chuck Thacker (Xerox PARC Alto co-creator). Anchors the story on the question of whether the Tablet PC will overcome the long pen-computing scrap heap (Apple Newton, AT&T EO, DEC Lectrice, Microsoft Pen Windows). Quotes Aberdeen Group analyst Peter Kastner: 'Microsoft will make a huge positioning mistake if this is viewed as a specialized information-gathering appliance.' Other voices: Bill Gates (Comdex 2001 keynote), Transmeta CTO David Ditzel, Parsons Corp CIO John Thomas, Lithonia Lighting director Lynn Brenton. Sets price at $2000-$2500/unit, names Adobe, Autodesk, Compaq, Intel, Transmeta among partners; ship target second half 2002.
importance_rationale: Marquee InformationWeek cover-story moment for Microsoft's Tablet PC; Kastner's positioning warning is prescient.
relevance_rationale: Direct Kastner quote at Aberdeen on a major Microsoft platform launch; complements other 2001-2002 Microsoft-related artifacts in the archive.
prescience_rationale: Kastner's warning that positioning Tablet PC as a 'specialized information-gathering appliance' would be a 'huge positioning mistake' was vindicated — Microsoft Tablet PC initiative stalled before iPad's 2010 launch redefined the category as general-purpose consumer/enterprise computing; the broader pen-computing thesis (digital ink, handwriting recognition, stylus + APIs) eventually paid off via Surface Pro and iPad Pro decades later.
